name: Tomato Upma

ingredients.name: fine rava
ingredients.notes: sooji or semolina or cream of wheat

steps.instruction: Heat a pan or kadai first. Add 1 cup rava or cream of wheat.
On a low heat, begin to roast the rava. Stir often while roasting the rava.
Switch off the heat and then add the roasted rava to a plate and keep aside.
In a pan, heat 2 tablespoons of ghee or oil on low to medium-low heat.
Add 1 teaspoon of mustard seeds. When you hear the crackling sound of mustard seeds, reduce the heat to a low.
Add 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal. Stir and saute them on a low heat till both the urad dal and chana dal become golden.
Now add 1 or 2 dry red chilies and a pinch of asafoetida (hing).
Add ⅓ cup of chopped onions. Saute the onions till they turn translucent.
Then add 1 or 2 green chilies (chopped), ½ inch ginger (chopped), 6 to 7 curry leaves (chopped).
Add ¾ cup of chopped tomatoes.
Add ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder and ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chilli powder. Saute the tomatoes till they soften.
Next add 2.5 cups of water. Add salt as per taste.
On a medium to high heat, let it come to a rolling boil.
When the water comes to a rolling boil, lower the flame to its lowest. Then add the rava in 4 to 5 batches.
Cover and allow the tomato upma to steam for 2 to 3 minutes on low heat.
Then switch off the heat. Lastly add about 2 tablespoons of chopped coriander leaves.
Drizzle ½ teaspoon of ghee on each serving and serve tomato upma hot.

tips: Use good quality fresh rava. Use the fine textured rava like Bombay rava.
Ensure that the rava is roasted well and evenly.
For some crunch you can add cashews or roasted peanuts to the dish.
Feel free to adjust the spices and seasonings as required.
This recipe can be scaled as per your needs.
variations: For a less spicy version, skip the red chilli powder.
You can also add ¼ to ½ teaspoon vangi bath powder or bisi bele bath powder instead of red chilli powder.
